Background

Education

Oberlin College and Conservatory (August 2019 - Present)

I anticipate receiving a B.A. degree with majors in Mathematics, History, and East Asian Studies from Oberlin College in May 2023. After graduation, I plan to pursue a PhD in Political Science with prospective focuses in methodology and comparative politics.

Bowling Green State University (May 2020 - September 2021)

During the summers of 2020 and 2021 I attended the Bowling Green State University Summer Session as a guest student, during which I took a significant amount of classes to either transfer or just supplement my studies at Oberlin.

Perrysburg High School (August 2015 - May 2019)

In 2019 I graduated from Perrysburg High School, a public high school in Perrysburg, Ohio, with summa cum laude recognition.

Research Experience

Michigan Technological University (June 2022 - Present)

Currently, I am working as a Research Assistant for Dr. Charles Wallace of the Michigan Technological University Computer Science Department. Primarily, my work entails declarative logic software development with Alloy and Spin for OECD Rules as Code-based project modeling Michigan statutory expungement law, MCL 780.621, which allows old convictions to be removed from individual’s criminal records. My work on the project also includes comprehensive research on the text and implications of the statute, utilizing linear temporal logic to write mathematical models of the statute, and conducting interviews with attorneys, judges, and paralegals on different interpretations of the statue.

Oberlin College and Conservatory (October 2021 - June 2022)

In 2021-2022 I worked as a Research Assistant on the Oberlin College History Design Lab’s African American Women Intellectuals project. As a member of the project team, I utilized the college archives to write scholarly biographies of female African American Oberlin graduates under the direction of Dr. Renee Romano.

Bowling Green State University (May 2021 - September 2021)

In the summer of 2021, I worked as a Research Assistant for Dr. Melissa Miller of the Bowling Green State University Political Science Department. As a member of the Trailblazing Women in Ohio Politics research team, I utilized archival newspaper records to compile a complete career history for women who have broken electoral barriers in Ohio. My research formed the basis of biographical narratives about US Representative Marcy Kaptur and former Ohio Attorney General and State Auditor Betty Montgomery that will appear on the project’s website in March 2023. My research was also utilized by Dr. Miller prior to interviewing each trailblazer for a public television documentary.

Teaching Experience

Oberlin College and Conservatory (September 2021 - Present)

Since 2021 I have worked as a Peer Tutor for Statistics, Chinese language, Data Structures, System Programming, and Discrete Mathematics.

Awards

Oberlin College and Conservatory (August 2019 - Present)

In 2019 I was awarded the John Frederick Oberlin Scholarship to pursue undergraduate studies at Oberlin College.

In 2021 and 2022 I was named an ITA (Intercollegiate Tennis Association) All-Academic Scholar and also made the NCAC (North Coast Athletic Conference) Honor Roll.

Skills

Languages

I am able to read, write, and speak Chinese with advanced proficiency.

Programming

Major projects I have undertaken utilized Bash Scripting, Python, R, C, and C++. My favorite editor for programming is Vim.

Office Suites

I am highly proficient in LaTeX and Markdown.

Supplements
